summary: The prior round's major finding is resolved. The implementation now reads the live body together with a body-edit version stamp, merges onto that fresh body, and only reports success when the post-write read proves exactly one edit occurred and the body matches. The formerly losing interleaving is detected by a two-edit delta with this writer's body still live and fails loudly without retrying; the new non-vacuous test specifically exercises that case and would fail under the prior body-only confirmation. A foreign edit strictly after this writer is distinguished by the foreign body being live and is safely re-merged with bounded retries. Read, edit, parse, and confirmation failures propagate nonzero, preserving fail-closed behavior. No reviewer-directed prompt injection was found in the diff.
